decisions.title: Replaced radial layout with structured grid rows
decisions.reasoning: Radial/polar layout scattered cards with no visible hierarchy — users couldn't parse relationships or priority. Structured grid rows using Gestalt proximity principles let whitespace communicate grouping and hierarchy without connection lines. The grid IS the information architecture.
decisions.source: commit b771140, then refined in 29d3e4e (brick grid sections)

decisions.title: Pivoted from dump-all-blocks to user-directed progressive disclosure
decisions.reasoning: The original model had the AI return 8-15 blocks at once for every query — overwhelming and no user agency. Progressive disclosure lets the user pull threads: type a query → get a seed card + prompt chips → click to explore deeper. The canvas grows as a tree of discoveries. User directs the investigation, not the AI. This also reduces AI response time (smaller focused responses) and makes the exploration path visible.
